title: Deep Temporal Reasoning in Video Language Models: A Cross-Linguistic Evaluation of Action Duration and Completion through Perfect Times
limitation_points:
* The study is not exhaustive regarding the full range of synonymous temporal constructions.
* The semi-synthetic, template-based approach may not fully reflect the variability found in natural language.
* The templates might not fully reflect spontaneous speech.
* The number of verb classes (157 classes for 400 videos) may not capture more nuanced actions.
* The gold standard accuracy might decrease when averaged across annotators with more annotations per language.

title: Amplifying Trans and Nonbinary Voices: A Community-Centred Harm Taxonomy for LLMs
limitation_points:
* The scope is limited to a US and English language context.
* The approach may fail to reflect non-Western gender practices.
* The approach may fail for non-English language TGNB users.
* The evaluation in Section 5.2 is a "toy example" that needs improvement.
* The evaluation only considers representational harms.
* The evaluation only considers creative prompts.
* Other LLMs models, such as ChatGPT, were not validated as evaluators due to an unacceptable rate of false positives.

title: Enhancing Human Evaluation in Machine Translation with Comparative Judgment
limitation_points:
* Practical considerations make it difficult to control all variables in human evaluation experiments.
* The single-sided MQM annotations were collected in 2023 with a different original goal, while the S×S MQM and S×S RR annotations were collected in 2024 for this project.
* Annotators were engaged in multiple projects throughout 2024 and thus may have performed other annotation tasks in between items collected for this project.
* The current work is not able to test MQM, S×S MQM, and S×S RR on a larger set of language pairs besides ZhEn and EnDe.
* It is impractical to exhaustively test all possible system pairs in the two tested language pairs.
* The study only focuses on pairwise side-by-side annotation, and does not explore comparing more than two outputs.
* The study does not investigate the impact of different annotation settings on annotator backgrounds, such as crowd-sourced workers.
* S×S MQM and S×S RR do not significantly increase the inter-annotator agreement of expert annotators.

title: Infogen: Generating Complex Statistical Infographics from Documents
limitation_points:
* Though Infogen delivers state-of-the-art performance, there is room for improvement in accurately determining and aligning sub-charts.
* Our dataset, Infodat, is relatively limited in size, which might affect the generalizability of the model in diverse domains like healthcare.
* The framework currently lacks support for customized template selection based on context, limiting its flexibility in tailoring infographics for specific user needs or varied contexts.

title: Partial Colexifications Improve Concept Embeddings
limitation_points: NO LIMITATIONS FOUND

title: Improved Unbiased Watermark for Large Language Models
limitation_points:
* There is an inherent trade-off between the robustness and detectability of our proposed MCMARK.
* Increasing the number of distribution channels can potentially enhance detectability but reduce robustness against attacks.
* Our proposed MCMARK is vulnerable to attacks where an adversary may modify output tokens to disrupt the watermark detection.

title: MaCP: Minimal yet Mighty Adaptation via Hierarchical Cosine Projection
limitation_points:
* The largest model tested was LLaMA2-13B, and experiments were not extended to larger models due to time constraints.
* A detailed analysis of domain-specific tasks, such as biomedical or legal text, was not conducted, which might require further tuning of the frequency partitioning strategy.
* The computational cost of calculating the DCT could be optimized further for extremely large-scale models.

title: Multi-Attribute Steering of Language Models via Targeted Intervention
limitation_points:
* The method may struggle in scenarios where the attributes to be aligned are highly complex or unsteerable.
* MAT-STEER relies on a small set of data to create steering vectors.
* Evaluations have so far been conducted on a select set of tasks and attributes chosen to be representative of standard steering objectives.
* MAT-STEER does not eliminate the risks associated with LLMs, such as bias and toxicity, entirely.

title: AdaptAgent: Adapting Multimodal Web Agents with Few-Shot Learning from Human Demonstrations
limitation_points:
* The best-performing agent attained an overall task success rate of less than 25% on both Mind2Web and VisualWebArena.
* There is significant room for improvement, particularly for tasks requiring long action sequences and websites with complex visual layouts.
* The proposed adaptation framework may be prone to failure in certain types of new tasks/websites/domains.
* The adapted agent’s performance may be sensitive to the selected few-shot demonstrations.

title: Can LLMs Identify Critical Limitations within Scientific Research? A Systematic Evaluation on AI Research Papers
limitation_points:
* The study does not include non-textual inputs such as figures, which are integral to many scientific papers.
* The study does not explore advanced RAG techniques, focusing instead on assessing the potential of LLM systems.
* The current benchmark covers a limited time span, which may not fully represent the evolving landscape of research in the field.
* The benchmark relies on an automated evaluation method, which could introduce inherent biases and affect the accuracy and reliability of the overall evaluation.
* The taxonomy and benchmark focus primarily on AI, and may not fully address the nuances of different scientific disciplines.
* The study's limited scope may introduce challenges and limitation types that the framework does not fully address, particularly in fields such as medicine, physics, and social sciences.

title: On the Acquisition of Shared Grammatical Representations in Bilingual Language Models
limitation_points:
* The language sample is not sufficiently diverse to draw strong, generalizable conclusions.
* The language sample is primarily driven by the availability of psycholinguistic datasets, which are more often representative of European languages.
* The models used are very small due to compute limitations.
* Training larger models may not have shown the same limits on shared representations and crosslingual transfer.
* Increasing the model size would likely be necessary to study successful crosslingual transfer in language pairs that are more different.
* Language data contamination could be a factor, with non-English data potentially contaminated with English data.
* English contamination in the Dutch data may be present due to language use or differences in accuracy of language identification methods.
* The models being small may limit the generalizability of the results to larger models.
* The limited compute budget restricted the number of manipulations that could be done.

title: Using Shapley interactions to understand how models use structure
limitation_points:
* The analysis is limited to pairwise interactions and does not consider higher-order interactions.
* The analysis is limited to correlational analyses and does not investigate causal relationships.
* The analysis is currently limited to pairwise features like syntactic proximity.
* The specifics of the results might not be relevant to state-of-the-art production models due to the difference in model size.
* The paper is limited to smaller models and has not been applied to larger models.

title: Unknown Title
limitation_points:
* The approach relies on access to logits for computing Equation (2), which many proprietary models restrict.
* Closed-source models that do not allow users to input raw token sequences inherently prevent adversarial tokenization attacks.

title: Classifying Unreliable Narrators with Large Language Models
limitation_points:
* The work focuses on short texts (no longer than 1050 tokens each), some of which do not contain complete beginnings, middles, and endings.
* The datasets are limited to texts written in English.
* The analysis for RQ1 in Section 6 is limited to only female, male, and other/ambiguous genders.
* The size of the dataset is relatively small due to the high cost of high-quality annotations.

title: CONCEPTCARVE: Dynamic Realization of Evidence
limitation_points:
* The Reddit data used in our experiments does not represent the full spectrum of online discussions, limiting the generalizability of our results to other platforms or domains.
* LLMs were used extensively for data annotation, which introduces potential biases inherent in these models.
* The overall quality and fairness of the labels may still be affected by the limitations of the LLMs themselves.
* LLMs have the potential for bias due to the nature of their training data.

title: QQSUM: A Novel Task and Model of Quantitative Query-Focused Summarization for Review-based Product Question Answering
limitation_points:
* We evaluated the textual quality of generated KPs only on AmazonQ&A, as it is the only public dataset with abundance of online community answers written by online users usable as ground truth for our automatic evaluation.
* Key points extracted from the Q&A answers might not fully be in line with viewpoints in reviews to answer questions.
* Opinions in product reviews may not sufficiently cover all opinions in community answers.

title: Navigating Rifts in Human-LLM Grounding: Study and Benchmark
limitation_points:
* System prompts can greatly shape the responses and provide specific guidance on the flow of dialogue, but were not used.
* RIFTS will only reflect tasks seen in WildChat due to the filtering process.
* Tasks in RIFTS depend on the LLMs used to serve WildChat (e.g. OpenAI LLMs).
* Forecasters implicitly learn what tasks fail for the GPT models deployed in WildChat.
* The annotator relies on GPT4o-mini to label logs with grounding acts and is not perfect.

title: Substance over Style: Evaluating Proactive Conversational Coaching Agents
limitation_points:
* The sample size in this study is limited to 31 end users and 11 health coach experts.
* The study only looks at user populations with extensive chatbot experience.
* All agents in this study leverage a single family of language models, Gemini.
* The study only focuses on a single domain of health coaching.

title: Open-World Planning via Lifted Regression with LLM-Inferred Affordances for Embodied Agents
limitation_points: NO LIMITATIONS FOUND

title: (RSA)2: A Rhetorical-Strategy-Aware Rational Speech Act Framework for Figurative Language Understanding
limitation_points:
* Limited dataset size and diversity
* Need for evaluating the framework on additional pragmatic phenomena such as metaphor and politeness
* Difficulty in generating relevant and non-overlapping alternative utterances
* Uncertainty in determining which rhetorical strategies are appropriate in a given context
* Need for a more principled approach to inducing rhetorical strategies

title: SYNTHIA: Novel Concept Design with Affordance Composition
limitation_points:
* Our work relies on human intuition to evaluate the novelty of the generated concepts.
* The model may still be subject to human bias and lack of coverage.
* The concept ontology does not cover every plausible concept category in the real-world.

title: Consistent Client Simulation for Motivational Interviewing-based Counseling
limitation_points:
* The framework suffers from prompt sensitivity, which may lead to error accumulation due to multiple steps of prompting.
* The design of effective prompts and their evaluation are important areas for further research.
* The paper focuses on client simulation only and does not develop highly competent counselor agents.
* The study is limited to a single dataset (AnnoMI) and does not involve more real-world counseling datasets.
* The study is restricted to the MI approach to counseling and does not accommodate other counseling approaches.

title: AUTALIC: A Dataset for Anti-AUTistic Ableist Language In Context
limitation_points:
* The dataset may still exhibit some selection bias due to reliance on keyword searches and specific social media threads for data collection.
* The work primarily reflects Western perspectives, and therefore the dataset may not be generalizable across languages and cultures.
* Anti-autistic and ableist speech may manifest and be perceived differently in different contexts, which is not accounted for in the dataset.
* The dataset consists of sentences sourced from Reddit, which may not be representative of all online platforms or communities.
* The search criteria included terms that may not exclusively target autism, such as "r*tard", which is a slur broadly used against people with intellectual disabilities.
* The inclusion of broader search terms like "neurodiver" returned unrelated content, such as discussions about the NEURODIVER video game.
* Most of the dataset's content was published in 2023, which may not be representative of ableist language used in other time periods.
* The dataset is relatively small, which may limit its ability to capture the full range of ableist language targeting autistic individuals.

title: Structural Reasoning Improves Molecular Understanding of LLM
limitation_points:
* The method relies on the accuracy of structural information in synthetic reasoning.
* Errors in molecule-backward reasoning could degrade performance.
* The method assumes that the given molecular representations are accurate.
* Real-world data can be noisy or incomplete.
* Extending the method to handle uncertain molecular inputs via self-correction remains an open challenge.

title: CAMI: A Counselor Agent Supporting Motivational Interviewing through State Inference and Topic Exploration
limitation_points:
* The topic tree component is not part of MI principles and an expert MI counselor may rely on experience to suggest topics.
* The data-driven topic tree construction is suboptimal.
* The framework currently relies on LLM prompting to perform state inference, topic exploration and strategy selection, which can lead to issues such as counselor responses being too lengthy and using too many questions.
* The study only focuses on MI-based counseling, and does not explore the integration of other counseling techniques, such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT).

title: Know You First and Be You Better: Modeling Human-Like User Simulators via Implicit Profiles
limitation_points:
* Scenario Applicability: Experiments were conducted on a single dataset, with minimal validation across others to confirm broader applicability.
* Linguistic and Cultural Scope: Our focus on English dialogues may limit the applicability of USP to other languages and cultural contexts.

title: Targeted Syntactic Evaluation for Grammatical Error Correction
limitation_points:
* The study has a relatively small number of minimal pairs per grammar item.
* CTSEG does not control the ratio of error operations (i.e., Missing, Replacement, Unnecessary).
* The distribution of error operations may differ from that of existing test sets, such as CoNLL-2014 and BEA-2019.
* The study only constructed a dataset for English, and not for other languages.
* The study did not enable fine-grained analysis by not constructing test sets with explicitly controlled error operation distribution for each grammar item.

title: VF-EVAL: Evaluating Multimodal LLMs for Generating Feedback on AIGC Videos
limitation_points:
* Only text-to-video models are considered, whereas videos generated from images may exhibit other types of error cases that are not addressed in this study.
* The design of the re-prompt pipeline is relatively simplistic, as it only incorporates textual feedback from humans.
* The specific positions of error cases are not included, which limits the granularity of the feedback.
* Cross-modal videos are not included in our dataset.
* The omission of cross-modal videos may overlook more complex scenarios that arise from multimodal interactions, since some video generation models also provide audio information.

title: Language Model Fine-Tuning on Scaled Survey Data for Predicting Distributions of Public Opinions
limitation_points:
* Language models cannot fully replace human involvement in the survey process.
* Language models remain limited in adapting to a rapidly changing world.
* Fine-tuning a language model on distributions of human opinions may inadvertently replicate and amplify existing biases of humans.
* A model fine-tuned on human opinions does not necessarily align with human values and behaviors.
* A model fine-tuned on human opinions does not serve as a perfect proxy for human decision-making.
* The scope of the work is restricted to language models prompted with group-level information generating response distributions.
* Survey response data remain subject to empirical variance, particularly for relatively small groups.
* The extent to which biases affect language models—and how best to address them—remains an open question.
* The finetuning approach has not explored fine-tuning with richer contextual information.
* Incorporating additional contextual details can improve the fidelity of model-generated opinions to actual human responses.

title: TESS 2: A Large-Scale Generalist Diffusion Language Model
limitation_points:
* Sampling speed can be improved by incorporating recent work in computer vision for single step sampling in diffusion-based models.
* Sampling speed can be improved by applying kernel or systems level optimizations.
* Sampling speed can be improved by investigating early stopping mechanisms.
* The model lags behind AR models on AlpacaEval and GSM8k when trained on Tulu 2 SFT data.
* The model is a weaker generalist model than its AR counterparts.
* Data quality can be improved to close the gap between the model and AR models.
* Continued pretraining of Mistral on Dolma results in degraded performance, suggesting that the adaptation data can be improved.
* A more thorough search and tuning of hyperparameters such as the learning rate can further improve performance.
* The model may display similar issues to autoregressive counterparts when it comes to producing toxic and biased content.
* Examining how results around toxic and harmful generations of autoregressive setups transfer to diffusion models remains an open area for future investigation and improvement.

title: KatFishNet: Detecting LLM-Generated Korean Text through Linguistic Feature Analysis
limitation_points:
* The scope of the KatFish benchmark is limited to three specific genres: essays, poetry, and paper abstracts.
* The study does not encompass all text types where LLM-generated content could present risks, such as news articles, social media posts, and legal documents.
* The study focuses on distinguishing between fully human-written and fully LLM-generated text, and does not account for hybrid content where human- and LLM-generated text are interwoven.
* The performance of detection methods that rely on linguistic features is impacted by the limitations of current Korean morphological analysis tools.

title: Uncovering the Impact of Chain-of-Thought Reasoning for Direct Preference Optimization: Lessons from Text-to-SQL
limitation_points:
* The databases provided by the Bird dataset are typically large, leading to significant time consumption when executing SQL queries.
* Resource contention among multiple processes can lead to SQL execution timeouts, causing correct predicted SQL queries to be incorrectly classified as incorrect, thereby introducing false negatives.
* The inaccuracy in feedback signals could potentially impact DPO training.
* Relying on execution results to distinguish between correct and incorrect SQL queries can lead to false positives, resulting in potentially unreliable feedback signals for DPO training.

title: On Generalization across Measurement Systems: LLMs Entail More Test-Time Compute for Underrepresented Cultures
limitation_points:
* The interplay between language and measurement systems and its impact on performance remains an important avenue for future research.
* The study only investigates how well LLMs generalize across measurement systems in English, which represents a ‘best-case’ scenario.
* LLMs are likely to mistranslate measurements more frequently and significantly in other languages, and incur higher inference costs due to tokenization into more subwords.
* The investigation only examines a selected subset of measurement systems and units.
* There are challenges in accessing comprehensive data, especially regarding systems still actively used within marginalized groups.
* There are more effective tools for retrieving facts and performing unit conversions than solely relying on LLMs, such as RAG systems or dedicated conversion tools.

title: CORDIAL: Can Multimodal Large Language Models Effectively Understand Coherence Relationships?
limitation_points:
* The benchmark is currently limited to analyzing coherence relations in single-turn discourses.
* There is a lack of publicly available datasets that provide multi-turn image-text pairs with annotated coherence relations.
* The benchmark is currently limited to the English language.

title: Veracity Bias and Beyond: Uncovering LLMs’ Hidden Beliefs in Problem-Solving Reasoning
limitation_points:
* We cannot fully explain the origins or causal mechanisms of Veracity Bias.
* The interplay between pretraining data, model architecture, and the emergence of biases remains unclear.
* Systematic methods to detect and characterize biases in LLMs remain limited.

title: Optimal Transport-Based Token Weighting scheme for Enhanced Preference Optimization
limitation_points:
* Our experiments were limited to off-policy and on-policy setups for direct preference optimization.
* We did not explore iterative on-policy setups due to limited computational resources.
* We conducted our experiments on relatively small models.
* The scalability and generalizability of our algorithms to larger models remain to be validated.
* We relied on GPT-4 as the evaluation judge, which may introduce potential biases and result in less accurate or reliable judgments.

title: LLM Meets Scene Graph: Can Large Language Models Understand and Generate Scene Graphs? A Benchmark and Empirical Study
limitation_points:
* The current textual narratives do not include object attributes (e.g., color, size)
* TSG Bench does not incorporate attributes in the scene graph generation process
* TSG Bench centers on a single actor, tasks do not involve additional individuals 
* The study does not integrate multi-modal approaches, such as utilizing Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to derive textual narratives from images or videos

title: Beyond Frameworks: Unpacking Collaboration Strategies in Multi-Agent Systems
limitation_points:
* The performance of the multi-agent system depends on the quality and completeness of the contextual data provided to each agent.
* Incomplete or ambiguous data can affect decision-making accuracy.
* Scalability issues may arise as the number of agents increases, with potential challenges in coordination and computational efficiency.
* The framework assumes agents can independently interpret context, which may be influenced by biases or lack of domain-specific knowledge.
* The generalization of the approach across different domains remains uncertain, as the tasks used are specific to certain contexts.
* The system does not integrate external knowledge, which could limit performance in dynamic or evolving scenarios.
* The interpretability of multi-agent decisions remains a challenge, as the rationale behind agent interactions can be difficult to trace.

title: The Invisible Hand: Unveiling Provider Bias in Large Language Models for Code Generation
limitation_points:
* The dataset cannot fully capture all potential biases present in complex real-world environments.
* The study does not quantify and compare the provider bias of different LLMs.
* The lack of access to the specific pre-training corpus and pipeline of LLMs limits an in-depth analysis of the exact sources of provider bias.
* The estimation relies on market share reports, which is not the reflection of real training data distribution.
* Accurately obtaining real training data distributions to analyze and pinpoint the sources of provider bias remains an open question.

title: K/DA: Automated Data Generation Pipeline for Detoxifying Implicitly Offensive Language in Korean
limitation_points:
* The model is demonstrated using open-source LLMs smaller than GPT-4 Turbo, and larger models may yield better consistency.
* The current dataset is primarily composed of Korean examples, limiting linguistic coverage.
* The pipeline has not been applied in diverse cultural and language settings for detoxification.

title: THOR-MoE: Hierarchical Task-Guided and Context-Responsive Routing for Neural Machine Translation
limitation_points:
* In this study, the design relies on the prior (the number of tasks and language groups), which may limit its extension to broader topics
* This work only conducts experiments on translation tasks and does not conduct experiments on other generation or discriminative tasks

title: Neuron Empirical Gradient: Discovering and Quantifying Neurons’ Global Linear Controllability
limitation_points:
* Tuning neuron values for skill-level output adjustment remains unexplored
* The current neuron linearity and gradient analysis focuses on single-token factual prompts, limiting its application to other domains and contexts.

title: Can Third Parties Read Our Emotions?
limitation_points:
* The observed limitations of third-party annotations are based on first-party labels and third-party annotations collected from study participants.
* First-party labels may be spurious and cannot be externally verified.
* The inclusion of potentially spurious first-party data points may have an impact on the overall findings.
* Some low-quality third-party annotations may still remain despite quality control measures.
* Inconsistencies in subjective judgment cannot be entirely eliminated.
* There is a lack of sufficient data within each demographic subgroup to conduct statistically robust analyses.
* The findings may not generalize to other demographic categorizations.
* The findings may not generalize to broader populations.
* The findings may not generalize to diverse cultural contexts.
* The findings may not generalize to different languages.

title: OZSpeech: One-step Zero-shot Speech Synthesis with Learned-Prior-Conditioned Flow Matching
limitation_points:
* The synthesized speech often exhibits slight distortions, which appear to contribute to a degradation in overall quality.
* Inaccuracies arise in the ground-truth data because phoneme durations are inherently real numbers rather than integers.
* The durations must be rounded to integer values, which subsequently degrades the quality of the synthesized speech in the temporal domain.
* The approach employed in this study presents a trade-off between synthesis quality and computational efficiency.
* Deepfake Fraud: Malicious entities may exploit these models to impersonate individuals, facilitating scams, misinformation, or fraudulent activities.
* Fabricated Media: Synthesized audio can be used to create misleading or defamatory content, influencing public perception and spreading misinformation.
* Privacy Violations: The unauthorized replication of voices without explicit consent raises ethical and legal concerns regarding individual privacy.
* Legal and Copyright Challenges: Certain voices may be subject to copyright, trademark, or publicity rights protections, potentially leading to legal disputes over their unauthorized use.

title: World Modeling Makes a Better Planner: Dual Preference Optimization for Embodied Task Planning
limitation_points:
* The current training and evaluation are conducted in a simulation environment, which may not fully capture the complexity and uncertainty of real-world scenarios, and may lead to the sim-to-real gap.
* The data collection pipeline requires additional computational resources due to the limitations in multimodal language models' critique capabilities.
* The framework's scalability is currently limited by the computational costs of the judge model, which may be reduced with advancements in vision-language models and embodied self-rewarding mechanisms.
* The deployment of similar systems in real-world environments may pose safety risks, particularly when handling potentially hazardous appliances, and requires comprehensive safety protocols and monitoring mechanisms.

title: JailbreakRadar: Comprehensive Assessment of Jailbreak Attacks Against LLMs
limitation_points:
* It is infeasible to evaluate all over 200 jailbreak attacks within a single paper.
* The research scope of the paper is limited to 17 representative attacks.
* The policies and corresponding datasets used are static and may become outdated as LLM-related policies evolve over time.
* Questions in the same category may trigger different responses from LLMs, which is not investigated in the paper.
* The automatic ASR evaluation method proposed is imperfect.
* The approach lacks domain knowledge to properly assess the quality of jailbroken responses or compare them with harmful knowledge from other sources.
* Involving LLMs in building a forbidden dataset might introduce unknown biases.
* Many jailbreak attacks involve using other LLMs for assistance, which could also introduce biases.
* Human annotation may still introduce some unavoidable biases.

title: CogniBench: A Legal-inspired Framework and Dataset for Assessing Cognitive Faithfulness of Large Language Models
limitation_points:
* Domain specificity: The benchmark centres on common-sense, open-domain dialogues and may not be applicable to high-stakes applications that demand domain expertise and finer-grained taxonomies of error.
* Legal analogy: The legal framework used to structure cognitive-faithfulness judgements may not be applicable across different jurisdictions, jurisprudence, and judicial discretion.
* Source coverage and bias: The use of Wikipedia as a starting point may introduce editorial biases that can skew reference distributions.

title: Neural Incompatibility: The Unbridgeable Gap of Cross-Scale Parametric Knowledge Transfer in Large Language Models
limitation_points:
* Current PKT methods rely on language for supervision
* The underlying principles behind PKT's effectiveness remain unclear
* It is uncertain why a stronger Ml yields no improvement
* Equipment limitations restricted experiments to models between 13B and 7B 
* Experiments have not been expanded to larger-scale LLMs

title: Enhancing Mathematical Reasoning in LLMs by Stepwise Correction
limitation_points:
* The method may not perform well for non-English tasks.
* The method is limited to solving problems where the answers are numerical values or entities.
* The study does not address multilingual mathematical reasoning tasks.
* The study does not address problems where the answers are neither numerical nor entity-based.

title: PsyDial: A Large-scale Long-term Conversational Dataset for Mental Health Support
limitation_points:
* The data is sourced from a single counseling platform in China, which may not be representative of other regions.
* The LLM judges tend to favor longer responses and assign higher scores, introducing a bias that must be addressed.
* The current study does not integrate multimodal models that combine video counseling dialogues, which could be a promising direction for future work.
* The study could benefit from expanding the data collection to multiple counseling platforms across different regions to achieve a more comprehensive data collection.

title: Enhancing Goal-oriented Proactive Dialogue Systems via Consistency Reflection and Correction
limitation_points:
* The framework relies on the closed-source GPT-4 as an annotator, raising concerns about the transparency and interpretability of the inconsistency detection process.
* The dependence on a proprietary model may limit the framework’s applicability and reproducibility in settings with restricted access.
* Achieving a balance between consistency and output diversity presents a challenge.
* Aiming for greater diversity may inadvertently introduce inconsistencies, complicating the framework’s objectives and potentially reducing response quality.

title: Exclusion of Thought: Mitigating Cognitive Load in Large Language Models for Enhanced Reasoning in Multiple-Choice Tasks
limitation_points:
* The research has not explored the application of EoT beyond multiple-choice questions.
* The research has not applied the core principles of systematic exclusion to areas such as knowledge graphs and retrieval-augmented generation.

title: Registering Source Tokens to Target Language Spaces in Multilingual Neural Machine Translation
limitation_points:
* Our limited computational resources restrict the number of supported languages to 24.
* The number of supported languages is fewer than that of M2M, NLLB, and commercial LLMs.
* Our training data includes fewer samples per translation direction than those used for training NLLB models.
* The Bridge Language strategy results in fewer supervised translation directions compared to NLLB.
* MITRE does not incorporate additional engineering strategies like back-translation and distillation.
* The use of registers introduces additional computational cost, doubling the number of source tokens.
* The training time for models with registers is increased by a factor of 1.34, 1.63, and approximately 1.01 compared to the vanilla decoder-only model, vanilla encoder-decoder model, and the previous state-of-the-art method, respectively.
* MITRE has not been evaluated for toxicity or undergone detoxification, limiting its recommended use to research purposes or applications with thorough processing.

title: VisuoThink: Empowering LVLM Reasoning with Multimodal Tree Search
limitation_points:
* The predictive rollout search process introduces significant computational overhead, making it potentially impractical for realtime applications.
* The approach particularly relies on tool interactions for stronger capability, which may require more effort in some specific deployment environments.
* The framework’s effectiveness is constrained by the quality of the base VLM’s reasoning capabilities - while it enhances performance, it cannot overcome fundamental model limitations.
* The evaluation focuses primarily on geometric and spatial reasoning tasks.

title: Automated CAD Modeling Sequence Generation from Text Descriptions via Transformer-Based Large Language Models
limitation_points: NO LIMITATIONS FOUND

title: LED-Merging: Mitigating Safety-Utility Conflicts in Model Merging with Location-Election-Disjoint
limitation_points:
* The framework has not been extended to heterogeneous architectures (e.g., cross-family model fusion)
* The framework has not been extended to multilingual scenarios
* There is a lack of standardized benchmarks for evaluating merged models
* Balancing safety and utility in multilingual model merging remains underexplored

title: DOLPHIN: Moving Towards Closed-loop Auto-research through Thinking, Practice, and Feedback
limitation_points:
* LLMs retain historical knowledge obtained from training data, which may cause knowledge leakage when generating ideas.
* Only the abstract and title are used in the idea generation process, which may result in LLM not being able to understand the technical details in the article and the logic between the articles.
* The code capabilities of LLMs are not sufficient to understand complex codes such as project-level codes, resulting in DOLPHIN being unable to verify complex tasks currently.

title: PerSphere: A Comprehensive Framework for Multi-Faceted Perspective Retrieval and Summarization
limitation_points:
* The dataset size could be further enhanced by adding irrelevant documents.
* The study does not address the challenge of retrieval from large data sets, which could be considered in future work.

title: Prompt-Guided Internal States for Hallucination Detection of Large Language Models
limitation_points:
* We heavily rely on the internal states of LLMs, which may be challenging to access in some cases, requiring us to use a proxy model.
* Our framework does not leverage other information produced by LLMs during text generation, such as probability information and the generated text itself.
* Our framework may inherit biases from the language model on certain issues, potentially leading to incorrect judgments.

title: Typology-Guided Adaptation in Multilingual Models
limitation_points:
* The model relies on hard routing based on MoI, which may not fully account for intermediate cases.
* The datasets cover only 10 Bantu languages out of over 500.
* Expanding to more languages is challenging due to limited digital resources and scarce structured annotations.
* MoI simplifies linguistic variation by focusing solely on noun class prefix statistics.
* Some Bantu languages exhibit irregular noun class behavior due to phonological changes, loanwords, or diachronic shifts that MoI does not capture.
* The work did not explore the applicability of MoI to other NLP tasks such as language modeling, parsing, or machine translation.
* The work focused on Transformer-based models, and the applicability of MoI to other architectures is unknown.
* The findings may not generalize universally to all 500 Bantu languages.
* Rigid typological categorizations could oversimplify linguistic diversity, particularly in languages with irregular noun class behavior or diachronic shifts.

* The use of sensitive data requires careful consideration of privacy, fairness, and ethical implications.
* Large language models can reinforce stereotypical patterns embedded in historical data, perpetuating harmful societal stereotypes.
* LLMs trained on biased data risk inadvertently amplifying these biases, undermining fairness and inclusivity in legal decision-making.

title: Are We in the AI-Generated Text World Already? Quantifying and Monitoring AIGT on Social Media
limitation_points:
* Limited coverage of LLMs: AIGTBench includes only 12 LLMs and does not cover all LLMs released across different time periods.
* The data set shows distributional bias favoring the GPT series 42.9% and the Llama series 39.05% models.
* Lack of analysis on multilingual platforms: The research focuses on English-dominated social media platforms.
* Insufficient dimensions of analysis across platforms: In-depth analysis of topic, engagement, and author is only conducted on the Medium platform, not on Quora and Reddit.

title: From English to Second Language Mastery: Enhancing LLMs with Cross-Lingual Continued Instruction Tuning
limitation_points:
* Due to limited resources, we conducted experiments using only one multilingual open-source parallel instruction dataset.
* Slight adjustments may be needed in the way parameters are automatically initialized in SPL if new data is introduced to replicate our method.
* The model’s sensitivity to assessing the difficulty of a batch of data through standard deviation may need to be adjusted.
* Considerable standard variance was observed in some tasks or language items when simulating low-resource scenarios by using different seed numbers for data sampling.
* It is challenging to ensure an even distribution of tasks during random sampling, leading to substantial result variance.

title: WET: Overcoming Paraphrasing Vulnerabilities in Embeddings-as-a-Service with Linear Transformation Watermark
limitation_points:
* The current design of the circulant transformation matrix is compromised if an attacker manages to recover any single row in the matrix.
* Using different weights for each row in the circulant matrix would mean losing crucial properties such as invertibility and full rank.
* The current design may not be optimal and can be potentially further improved.
* The focus on simple classification tasks may not be sufficient to fully validate embedding quality.
* The current study does not explore other more complex NLP tasks, such as retrieval and generation, to gain a deeper understanding of the true impact of introducing watermarks into embeddings.

title: HoPE: A Novel Positional Encoding Without Long-Term Decay for Enhanced Context Awareness and Extrapolation
limitation_points:
* The method has only been implemented using the vanilla attention mechanism, with no exploration of other attention mechanism variants.
* There is a potential risk of misuse for generating harmful content.
* There is a potential risk of perpetuation of biases.
* There are data privacy concerns.
* There are environmental costs linked to computational resource consumption.

title: One QuantLLM for ALL: Fine-tuning Quantized LLMs Once for Efficient Deployments
limitation_points:
* For bit-width exceeding 4 bits, the quantization method becomes lossless.
* For bit-width less than 2 bits, there is a significant loss in accuracy.
* The mixed-precision setting is not flexible and can only be selected from 2, 3, and 4 bits.
* The current 2-bit quantization still suffers from remarkable accuracy loss even when equipped with LoRA tuning.
* The quantization error causes troubles in employment and training.
* An orthogonal approach is needed to alleviate the quantization error.

title: Beyond Logits: Aligning Feature Dynamics for Effective Knowledge Distillation
limitation_points:
* Our proposed FDD method utilizes the LM head to project intermediate layer representations of both the teacher and student models into a shared vocabulary space, which may restrict its applicability when applied across different model architectures or vocabulary configurations.
* Due to limited computational resources, our experiments were conducted with a teacher model of up to 13 billion parameters and a student model of up to 7 billion parameters.
* The limitation in model size prevents us from verifying the generalizability of our findings to extremely large models, such as those with 405 billion parameters.

title: Native Sparse Attention: Hardware-Aligned and Natively Trainable Sparse Attention
limitation_points:

title: Does the Emotional Understanding of LVLMs Vary Under High-Stress Environments and Across Different Demographic Attributes?
limitation_points:

title: S2WTM: Spherical Sliced-Wasserstein Autoencoder for Topic Modeling
limitation_points:
* Computing SSW requires multiple spherical Radon transforms, leading to increased computational cost as the number of projections grows.
* LLMs are known to exhibit biases.
* The topic models learn patterns from data without explicit supervision, and their outputs may reflect biases present in the dataset.
* Any unintended biases in the model outputs are acknowledged as part of the limitations of automated topic modeling.

title: Learning to Look at the Other Side: A Semantic Probing Study of Word Embeddings in LLMs with Enabled Bidirectional Attention
limitation_points:
* The underlying mechanisms of how activating bidirectional attention impacts the semantic encoding of word representations remain unclear.
* The correspondence between high-dimensional dense vectors in computational models and semantic information warrants further investigation.
* The study is limited to the English language with the Sheared-Llama-1.3B and Llama2-7B models.
* The study did not test bigger models due to limitation of computational resources.
* Different languages and models may yield varying effects on the performance of lexical semantic tasks.
* The approach to extracting contextual word representations may not be the optimal one for each model.
* The inherent capabilities of bidirectional attention mechanisms were not thoroughly evaluated in the study.
* The study did not evaluate text generation capabilities.
* Bidirectional attention mechanisms may significantly exacerbate text repetition in LLMs.
* Enabling bidirectional attention in LLMs may disrupt the LLM's native autoregressive generation, causing it to resemble BERT-like models that struggle with coherent text production.

title: Tracing and Dissecting How LLMs Recall Factual Knowledge for Real World Questions
limitation_points:
* Our analysis primarily focuses on commonsense reasoning tasks involving factual knowledge recall, and the identified patterns may not fully generalize to other types of reasoning tasks.
* Our backtracing methodology operates under the assumption that only sparse connections between reasoning tokens are significant, which may potentially overlook some subtle information flows within the network.
* We acknowledge the inherent challenge of mapping complex neural mechanisms to human-understandable explanations, potentially leading to oversimplification of the actual processes involved.

title: Employing Discourse Coherence Enhancement to Improve Cross-Document Event and Entity Coreference Resolution
limitation_points:

* The upstream task span detection is also important for coreference resolution.
* The tasks of CD-DCE and CDCR are performed in a pipeline, which will lead to the accumulation of cascading errors.
* Our model only works on monolingual and single-modal datasets.

title: Data Whisperer: Efficient Data Selection for Task-Specific LLM Fine-Tuning via Few-Shot In-Context Learning
limitation_points:

* The method may not be directly applicable in some commercial API-driven models (e.g., GPT-4 and DeepSeek-R1).
* Further research is needed to explore the performance of Data Whisperer with larger LLMs and datasets.
* The current evaluations do not offer insights into the scalability of the method and its potential in extreme-scale scenarios.

title: Synthesizing Post-Training Data for LLMs through Multi-Agent Simulation
limitation_points:

title: SoftCoT: Soft Chain-of-Thought for Efficient Reasoning with LLMs
limitation_points:

title: FCMR: Robust Evaluation of Financial Cross-Modal Multi-Hop Reasoning

title: Beyond Prompt Engineering: Robust Behavior Control in LLMs via Steering Target Atoms
limitation_points:

* The limited availability of publicly accessible SAEs restricts the experiments to primarily the Gemma-2-9B-pt, Gemma2-9B-it models and Llama-3.1-8B.
* The method's generalizability and scalability to a broader range of LLMs, including larger and more diverse architectures, remains to be validated.
* It is not possible to exhaustively enumerate all possible prompts or prove that the chosen prompts are the optimal choices.
* The applicability of STA to other nuanced domains, such as multi-turn dialogue or complex reasoning tasks, remains to be validated in future work.

title: MobiLoRA: Accelerating LoRA-based LLM Inference on Mobile Devices via Context-aware KV Cache Optimization
limitation_points:

* Our proof-of-concept implementation focuses on a specific mobile device platform with only GPU acceleration.
* Commercial-off-the-shelf mobile devices have variant hardware configurations, such as some mobile platforms relying on a domain-specific accelerator, such as a neural processing unit (NPU).

title: Language Models Resist Alignment: Evidence From Data Compression
limitation_points:

* The phenomenon's universal applicability, such as in multimodal models, has not been investigated
* The relationship between model elasticity and scaling laws has not been theoretically uncovered
* The amount of training data required for elasticity to manifest has not been determined
* The quantitative effect of increasing model parameters and pre-training data volume on elasticity has not been analyzed

title: Beyond the Answer: Advancing Multi-Hop QA with Fine-Grained Graph Reasoning and Evaluation
limitation_points:

title: Mamba Knockout for Unraveling Factual Information Flow
limitation_points:
* Our use of attention knockout is limited by its application in continuous layer windows, with all SSMs in a layer affected simultaneously.
* The approach may fail to recognize a more diffuse and intricate pattern of information flow that could occur in reality.
* The method does not elaborate on what information actually passes through the critical connections between tokens.
* The attention knockout effect is not ecological, and any change in probability could be attributed to it.
* Attention-knockout focuses only on the flow of information between tokens, and cannot be used to explain the role of token-independent operations.
* The methods do not shed light on why both Transformers and Mamba models converge to similar patterns of information flow.
* The approach does not explain the inductive-biases of the different architectures that drive the differences in information flow.

title: Small Changes, Big Impact: How Manipulating a Few Neurons Can Drastically Alter LLM Aggression
limitation_points:

* Limited Understanding of the Mechanisms by Which Aggression Neurons Emerge During Training, including how these neurons emerge during the training process and which data or training mechanisms play a critical role in forming aggression neurons.

title: Finding Needles in Images: Can Multimodal LLMs Locate Fine Details?
limitation_points:

title: Generating Plausible Distractors for Multiple-Choice Questions via Student Choice Prediction
limitation_points:

* The distractor generator occasionally produces invalid distractors, necessitating review by human experts or high-performing LLMs to accurately evaluate students’ knowledge.

title: Exploring Explanations Improves the Robustness of In-Context Learning
limitation_points:
* X2-ICL is not directly applicable to open-ended tasks and may be inefficient when handling tasks with an excessively large label set.
* X2-ICL exhibits lower performance on in-distribution datasets while demonstrating strong robustness to OOD datasets, and addressing this performance trade-off remains an open challenge.
* The practice of single-label assignment in standard NLI datasets may amplify distributional differences between datasets due to inherent label ambiguity.
* X2-ICL requires models with advanced reasoning capabilities to function effectively.
* X2-ICL incurs higher costs than ICL and X-ICL due to the comprehensive reasoning during inference.

title: Prediction Hubs are Context-Informed Frequent Tokens in LLMs
limitation_points:
* The empirical finding that hubs reflect context-dependent frequency distributions only holds for the models experimented with and should be extended to other model families and sizes.
* There is a lack of understanding of how prediction hubs come about.
* The nature of the distance distributions of some models and the reason why they lead to nuisance hubs will have to be studied in future work.
* The understanding of when a hub is a “junk” item or semantically distant from the items it is a near neighbour of is entirely based on qualitative observation and needs to be turned into automated quantitative scores.

title: Capability Salience Vector: Fine-grained Alignment of Loss and Capabilities for Downstream Task Scaling Law

* The experiments are limited to six popular objective evaluation benchmarks.
* Exploration of more challenging tasks or subjective evaluations is left for future work.

title: CRUXEVAL-X: A Benchmark for Multilingual Code Reasoning, Understanding and Execution
limitation_points:

* The model-based translation method cannot guarantee that all data will be perfectly translated into other programming languages.
* Translated codes from Python cannot reflect several specific language features of other languages, making it challenging to create highly aligned multilingual data.

title: Graph of Records: Boosting Retrieval Augmented Generation for Long-context Summarization with Graphs
limitation_points:

* The simulated queries may not accurately reflect the real-world distribution, as they do not account for the possibility of users asking many meaningless questions.

title: Rubrik’s Cube: Testing a New Rubric for Evaluating Explanations on the CUBE dataset
limitation_points:
* The scoring strategy used is binary, categorising explanations as either good or bad, and does not reflect varying degrees of quality.
* A more nuanced scoring system, such as a Likert scale, requires considerably more research to develop a valid and reliable graded scale.
* The approach does not explicitly assess the quality of reasoning itself, only the quality of explanations.
* The study only includes datasets in English, and it is possible that some attributes of a good explanation may change depending on cultural heritage, literature, and history in other languages.
* The concept of explanations may differ depending on the linguistic community, which may influence how explanation types, components, or dimensions are prioritised or understood.
* The assigned probabilities by LLM annotators often failed to sum to 100%, indicating a lack of consistent or meaningful probability assignment, and were not considered in the data analysis.
* The study lacks the means to make meaningful comparisons between human and LLM annotator confidence levels.

title: A Dual-Mind Framework for Strategic and Expressive Negotiation Agent
limitation_points:
* The DMNA framework's performance may be limited in multi-party negotiations due to the need to coordinate and optimize negotiation goals in real time with multiple participants.
* The framework may be limited in cross-cultural negotiations due to differences in cultural backgrounds that can lead to varying interpretations of the same strategy.
* The DMNA framework needs to enhance its ability to process non-textual information such as visual and auditory cues to better meet the demands of complex negotiation scenarios involving multimodal interactions.

title: Ref-Long: Benchmarking the Long-context Referencing Capability of Long-context Language Models
limitation_points:

* The evaluation may introduce potential bias in the results.
* The SummHay benchmark covers only a limited set of topics, which may not fully capture the performance of LCLMs across a broader range of topics.
* The effectiveness of Ref-Long in scenarios involving topics beyond those covered by SummHay remains uncertain.
* Model-generated contents may contain toxic or harmful information, necessitating comprehensive data processing by users.
* The original benchmark used for Ref-LongF may contain toxic or harmful information, despite manual review.

title: Revisiting Scaling Laws for Language Models: The Role of Data Quality and Training Strategies
limitation_points:
* Model Dependency: The results and scaling laws derived from our experiments are based on specific model architectures and configurations, and may not universally apply to all types of models.
* Over-Training Focus: The study predominantly focuses on over-training, and does not extensively explore other critical factors such as underfitting, model robustness, and generalization across different tasks and domains.
* Limited validation: The conclusions are not validated using recent, high-quality pretraining datasets.
* Limited scalability testing: The experiments are not performed on larger models with more training tokens to ensure the scalability and robustness of the findings.

title: Limited Generalizability in Argument Mining: State-Of-The-Art Models Learn Datasets, Not Arguments
limitation_points:
* This study did not separate direct from implicit arguments lacking clear structural and lexical cues, including discourse markers.
* The study assumed implicit arguments are rare, which may affect interpretation.
* Some datasets require granted access.
* The extraction scripts were not always available, and procedures were derived from documentation and understanding of the original process.
* The use of spaCy2 for sentence boundary extraction may produce boundaries that differ from the original assumptions.
* The reproducibility of the experiments may be constrained by factors such as data size, runtime, and associated costs.

title: Enhancing Machine Translation with Self-Supervised Preference Data
limitation_points:

title: Unveil: Unified Visual-Textual Integration and Distillation for Multi-modal Document Retrieval

* The processing time may be affected, especially when dealing with large volumes of documents.

title: Don’t Get Lost in the Trees: Streamlining LLM Reasoning by Overcoming Tree Search Exploration Pitfalls

title: MEXMA: Token-level objectives improve sentence representations

title: Uncertainty-Aware Iterative Preference Optimization for Enhanced LLM Reasoning

title: AgentDropout: Dynamic Agent Elimination for Token-Efficient and High-Performance LLM-Based Multi-Agent Collaboration

title: Towards Dynamic Theory of Mind: Evaluating LLM Adaptation to Temporal Evolution of Human States

title: GRaMPa: Subword Regularisation by Skewing Uniform Segmentation Distributions with an Efficient Path-counting Markov Model
limitation_points:
* Due to computational constraints, the vocabulary size |V | = 32768 and the BPE/GRaMPa multiplexing rate p = 0.5 were not tuned.
* The hyperparameter search was not extensive, and GRaMPa's performance could likely be improved with more tuning.
* The stochastic ULM baseline's performance could also be improved with a more extensive hyperparameter search.
* The optimal value of ℓmin is not known, and increasing it from 1 to 2 had a positive impact, but the upwards trend may stop or reverse at some point.
* The introduction of multiple hyperparameters in GRaMPa may be a challenge for practitioners.
* The model was not trained in a high-resource setting, only with 6-layer DeBERTa models on a 50k truncated corpus.
* The investigation of how GRaMPa scales to larger models and datasets is left to future work.
* The effect of making the stochastic tokenisers deterministic at inference time was not tested.
* The models were not fine-tuned with different tokenisers during pre-training and inference.
* The models were not pre-trained with p = 0 or p = 1, which would have led to additional experiments.
* The fine-tuning results were generated using only 5 hyperparameter grid samples per model-task pair, which may not be enough to be confident about the reported best.
* The theoretical results sometimes assume an infinite vocabulary, which does not model the constraints of finite vocabularies.

title: Evaluating the Evaluation of Diversity in Commonsense Generation
